title: Case Study: Harvard University Data Breach and Identity-Centric Threats in Modern Cybersecurity
abstract: Research analyzing the 2025–2026 Harvard University data breach, focusing on identity-centric attack vectors such as vishing, adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM), and MFA bypass techniques. Introduces the Identity Exploitation Model (IEM) and applies MITRE ATT&CK and STRIDE frameworks to evaluate systemic weaknesses in modern authentication systems. Published as open-access preprint.
note: Note: This work is part of an ongoing long-term research project on identity-based cyberattacks. These publications are early case studies released to deepen understanding and refine the broader research direction.

title: Case Study: Princeton University Data Breach and Its Cybersecurity Implications in Higher Education
abstract: Research analyzing the November 2025 Princeton University vishing breach using STRIDE and MITRE ATT&CK frameworks. Covers attack methodology, institutional response, and a multi-layered defense framework for higher education. Published as open-access preprint.
